title: RF-Benchmark-Hub leaderboard result
description: One evaluation run = one result.json = one leaderboard row: a single (task, model, regime, dataset, split, track) evaluation. Emitted ONLY by `rfbench eval` and validated against this schema before it may enter leaderboard/results/<task>/*.json. The regime is ALWAYS declared, never inferred (D5). Per-task metric keys stay normative in docs/EVALUATION_PROTOCOL.md: `metrics.values` is an open scalar map with a `primary` pointer, so adding a task/metric bumps only the task `version`, never this frozen schema. Closed objects use additionalProperties=false to catch typos; only genuinely open maps (metrics.values, metrics.curves, eval.conditions, per_class) stay open.

schema_version.description: SemVer of THIS result schema. Frozen at Sprint-0; consumers pin the major. Lets the leaderboard reader migrate old rows.

result_id.description: Optional stable slug identifying this row, unique within a task (used as the leaderboard/results/<task>/<result_id>.json filename stem).

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when the result was emitted by the harness.

task.description: Which canonical task and protocol version this result scores. `version` bumps when EVALUATION_PROTOCOL.md changes a metric or split (a breaking change).

name.description: Registered task id (matches rfbench/tasks/<name>/ and the task registry).

version.description: Task protocol version from EVALUATION_PROTOCOL.md. Any metric/split change bumps this. Matches the '-v<N>' suffix of canonical_split_id.

model.description: Identity of the evaluated model. Only `name` is required for a self_reported row; the rest attribute and enrich the board.

family.description: Coarse bucket for the board: 'baseline' (seeds the board) or 'foundation' (FM wrapper exposing embed()).

n_params.description: Total parameter count of the evaluated model, including any adapter head (Model.n_params).

backbone.description: Optional backbone/checkpoint id for foundation models (e.g. a pretrained RF encoder or EMA tag).

pretrained.description: Whether the backbone weights were pretrained before applying the regime.

url.description: Optional link to a model card, paper, or repo.

regime.description: The adaptation regime (D5). ALWAYS declared, NEVER inferred. The board never mixes regimes in one column. `k_shot` is REQUIRED iff name=='few_shot' and FORBIDDEN otherwise (enforced by allOf).

name.description: One of the four locked regimes.

k_shot.description: Examples per class. REQUIRED for few_shot only.

dataset.description: Concrete dataset variant the split was drawn from.

split.description: The canonical, deterministic split actually evaluated. `canonical_split_id` + `checksum` tie the row to versioned split indices under leaderboard/splits/<dataset>/ (no raw data). `track` names the sub-protocol / evaluation condition where a task defines several; SEI reports closed_set/cross_receiver/cross_day (and open_set) as SEPARATE rows via this field, so the board never blends them.

track.description: Sub-protocol / evaluation condition. Free-form so Wave-B tasks add tracks without a schema bump. SEI: closed_set|cross_receiver|cross_day|open_set. Wideband detection: detection|recognition. AMC/sensing typically omit it (single closed-set track).

seed.description: Seed used to generate the split (42 by protocol convention).

checksum.description: SHA-256 of the split-index file in leaderboard/splits/<dataset>/*.idx.json. Ties the score to exact indices.

metrics.description: Computed metrics for this task. `primary` names the ranking metric (== Metric.primary_key) and MUST be a key of `values`. `values` is an OPEN scalar map so new tasks/metrics need no schema change; which scalars are MANDATORY per task stays normative in EVALUATION_PROTOCOL.md and is enforced by the task's Metric + `rfbench submit --check` (AMC: accuracy_overall + macro_f1; SEI closed-set: rank1_accuracy; SEI open-set: auroc, eer; detection: mAP, mAR, IoU; sensing: pd@pfa=0.1, latency_ms). `curves` holds non-scalar reportables (accuracy_vs_snr, ROC).

primary.description: Name of the primary (ranking) metric; MUST also appear as a key in `values`. Per-task primaries (EVALUATION_PROTOCOL.md): amc->accuracy_overall, sei(closed)->rank1_accuracy, sei(open)->auroc, wideband_detection->mAP, spectrum_sensing->pd@pfa=0.1.

values.description: Scalar metric name -> value. Keys are drawn from the task protocol; `primary` MUST be present here. Documented keys are listed as properties but the map stays open (additionalProperties number) for extensibility.

curves.description: Optional non-scalar reportables keyed by name (e.g. 'accuracy_vs_snr' with x=snr_db,y=accuracy; 'roc' with x=pfa,y=pd). Each is an array of {x,y[,label]} points. Not used for ranking.

per_class.description: Optional per-class scalar scores keyed by class label.

eval.description: Full-protocol conditions of the run (EVALUATION_PROTOCOL.md 'Common rules': recorded in result.json.eval.conditions). Optional for a bare Tier-1 self_reported dump but REQUIRED for a Tier-2 verifiable submission (checked by `rfbench submit --check`). AMC MUST record the full SNR range (no cherry-picking) here.

conditions.description: Open map of protocol conditions guarding comparability. AMC: {snr_db_min, snr_db_max, full_snr_range:true}. Sensing: {pfa:0.1}. Detection: {iou_threshold}. Kept open for extensibility.

n_samples.description: Number of evaluated samples in the split.

environment.description: Reproducibility fingerprint captured by evaluate(): seed, hardware, and software env. Optional for Tier-1; a Tier-2 submission also carries the full manifest (submission.schema.json) referenced by `submission_ref`.

hardware.description: Free-form hardware string, e.g. '4x NVIDIA GB200'.

code_commit.description: git SHA of the code that produced this result.

submission_ref.description: Optional repo-relative path to the submission manifest (submission.schema.json) backing this result. Required by CI before a result may become 'verified'.

verification.description: Two-tier verification state (D4 / SUBMISSION.md). Starts 'self_reported'; a maintainer re-run within tolerance flips it to 'verified'. verified_* fields are REQUIRED iff status=='verified' (enforced by allOf), so a verified row can never lack provenance.

status.description: 'self_reported' (emitted locally, Tier-1) or 'verified' (maintainer re-ran within tolerance, Tier-2).

verified_by.description: Maintainer handle who re-ran and signed off.

verified_date.description: ISO date of the verified re-run.

method.description: How the maintainer reproduced: 'eval_only' (weights provided, default) or 'full_retrain' (seed baselines).

tolerance.description: Absolute tolerance on the primary metric the re-run matched within.

observed_primary.description: Primary-metric value the maintainer measured on re-run, compared to the submission's expected_metrics within tolerance.
